title: Workspace list page loads after login
description: Verifies an authenticated user can log in and the Workspaces list page loads.

steps.description: Verify text "Add New Workspace" is visible

title: Create a new workspace
description: Verifies a user can open the create dialog, enter a title, and see the new workspace appear in the list.

steps.description: Verify text "Add New Workspace" dialog is visible
Type "My Test Workspace" into the Title field
Verify text "My Test Workspace" is visible

title: Create a workspace then edit its title
description: Verifies a user can create a workspace and then successfully update its title via the Edit dialog.

steps.description: Type "Edit Me Workspace" into the Title field
Verify text "Edit Me Workspace" is visible
Click the "Edit" button on the "Edit Me Workspace" card
Clear the Title field and type "Renamed Workspace"

title: Create a workspace then delete it
description: Verifies a user can create a workspace and then delete it, removing it from the list.

steps.description: Type "Delete Me Workspace" into the Title field
Verify text "Delete Me Workspace" is visible
Click the "Delete" button on the "Delete Me Workspace" card
Verify text "Delete Me Workspace" is not visible

title: Create a workspace then open it by clicking the card
description: Verifies that clicking on a workspace card navigates to the workspace detail page.

steps.description: Type "Open Me Workspace" into the Title field
Verify text "Open Me Workspace" is visible
Click on the "Open Me Workspace" card title
Verify text "Open Me Workspace" is visible in the sidebar

title: Open workspace with no nodes shows empty state
description: Verifies that opening the NoNodes workspace displays the 'No nodes yet' empty state.

steps.description: Click on the "Workspaces" tab in the top navigation
Verify text "No nodes yet" is visible
Verify text "Create first node" is visible

title: Create first node in empty workspace
description: Verifies a user can create the first node in an empty workspace and see it appear in the sidebar.

steps.description: Click on the "Workspaces" tab in the top navigation
Verify text "No nodes yet" is visible
Verify text "Create Root Node" is visible
Type "My First Node" into the Title field
Verify text "My First Node" is visible in the sidebar

title: Open workspace with existing node shows it in sidebar
description: Verifies that opening the HasNodes workspace shows the existing RootNode in the sidebar.

steps.description: Click on the "Workspaces" tab in the top navigation
Verify text "RootNode" is visible in the sidebar

title: Click a node in sidebar navigates to node route
description: Verifies that clicking a node in the sidebar changes the URL to the node's route.

steps.description: Click on the "Workspaces" tab in the top navigation
Verify text "RootNode" is visible in the sidebar
Verify URL has changed and contains a node path segment after the workspace ID

title: Chat panel shows empty state when no node selected
description: Verifies that opening a workspace without selecting a node shows the 'No node selected' empty state in the chat panel.

steps.description: Verify text "No node selected" is visible
Verify text "Select a node from the sidebar to start a conversation." is visible

title: Selecting a node shows chat interface with input
description: Verifies that clicking a node in the sidebar shows the chat area with an input and the 'Start a conversation' empty state.

steps.description: Verify text "Start a conversation" is visible
Verify text "Ask anything about this topic" is visible
Verify the chat input with placeholder "Ask me anything ..." is visible

title: Send a chat message and receive a response
description: Verifies that typing a message and submitting it in the chat sends it and the assistant replies.

steps.description: Verify the chat input with placeholder "Ask me anything ..." is visible
Type "Hello, say hi back" into the chat input
Press Enter or click the submit button to send the message
Verify text "Hello, say hi back" is visible as a user message
Verify an assistant response message appears (wait up to 30 seconds for streaming to complete)

title: Notes panel shows empty state when no node selected
description: Verifies that switching to the Notes view without a node selected shows 'No node selected' and the helper text.

steps.description: Click the tab button with aria-label "Show notes only" in the workspace header to switch to Notes view
Verify text "No node selected" is visible in the notes panel
Verify text "Select a node to view its notes." is visible

title: Selecting a node in notes view shows Read Only header
description: Verifies that after switching to Notes view and clicking a node in the sidebar, the notes panel header appears showing 'Read Only' status.

steps.description: Click the tab button with aria-label "Show notes only" to switch to Notes view
Click on "RootNode" in the sidebar to select the node
Verify text "Read Only" is visible in the notes panel header
Verify the notes panel is visible (either "No note yet" or the editor content area is shown)

title: Toggle edit mode in notes panel shows Editing status
description: Verifies that toggling the edit switch in the notes panel header changes the status from 'Read Only' to 'Editing' and shows the editor placeholder.

steps.description: Click the tab button with aria-label "Show notes only" to switch to Notes view
Click on "RootNode" in the sidebar to select the node
Verify text "Read Only" is visible in the notes panel header
Click the "Edit" label or the toggle switch (id="edit-mode-toggle") to enable edit mode
Verify text "Editing" is visible in the notes panel header (replacing "Read Only")
Verify the notes editor content area is visible and the "No note yet" empty state is gone

title: Type text into notes editor and verify it appears
description: Verifies that after enabling edit mode, a user can click into the Lexical rich-text editor, type text, and see it rendered in the editor.

steps.description: Click the tab button with aria-label "Show notes only" to switch to Notes view
Click on "RootNode" in the sidebar to select the node
Click the "Edit" label or the toggle switch (id="edit-mode-toggle") to enable edit mode
Verify text "Editing" is visible in the notes panel header
Click into the notes editor contenteditable area (the rich-text editor with placeholder "Start writing…")
Type "Hello from the notes editor" into the editor
Verify text "Hello from the notes editor" is visible in the notes editor area

title: Suggestion chips appear after 3 chat exchanges
description: Verifies that after creating a workspace and node from scratch and completing at least 3 full assistant exchanges, follow-up suggestion chips appear below the last message.

steps.description: Type "SuggestTest Workspace" into the Title field
Click on the "SuggestTest Workspace" card title to open it
Click the "Create first node" button to open the create node dialog
Verify text "Create Root Node" is visible
Type "Python Overview" into the Title field
Verify text "Python Overview" is visible in the sidebar
Click on "Python Overview" in the sidebar to select the node
Verify the chat input with placeholder "Ask me anything ..." is visible
Type "What is Python?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Verify the user message "What is Python?" is visible in the chat
Wait for the first assistant response to fully appear (wait up to 45 seconds for streaming to complete and status to return to idle)
Wait 3 seconds for the UI to settle before sending the next message
Type "What are Python's main use cases?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Verify the user message "What are Python's main use cases?" is visible in the chat
Wait for the second assistant response to fully appear (wait up to 45 seconds for streaming to complete)
Wait 3 seconds for the UI to settle before sending the next message
Type "How does Python compare to JavaScript?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Verify the user message "How does Python compare to JavaScript?" is visible in the chat
Wait for the third assistant response to fully appear (wait up to 45 seconds for streaming to complete)
Wait 3 seconds for suggestion generation to complete
Verify suggestion chips are visible below the last assistant message (look for clickable pill-shaped buttons or a branch suggestion card with a git branch icon)

title: Dismiss button hides suggestion chips
description: Verifies that after creating a workspace and node from scratch and getting suggestions, clicking the X dismiss button hides the suggestions.

steps.description: Verify URL contains "/workspaces" and the text "Workspaces" is visible on the page (wait up to 10 seconds for the page to fully load)
Verify the "Add New Workspace" button is visible and interactable
Verify the workspace creation dialog is open and a Title input field is visible
Type "DismissTest Workspace" into the Title input field
Verify the Title field contains "DismissTest Workspace"
Click the "Create" button in the dialog and wait up to 5 seconds for the dialog to close
Verify text "DismissTest Workspace" is visible in the workspaces list
Click on the "DismissTest Workspace" card title to open it
Verify URL contains "/workspaces/" and the workspace view has loaded
Verify the "Create first node" button is visible
Verify the node creation dialog is open with a Title input field visible
Type "Python Overview" into the Title field
Click the "Create" button in the dialog
Verify text "Python Overview" is visible in the sidebar
Click on "Python Overview" in the sidebar to select the node
Verify the chat input with placeholder "Ask me anything ..." is visible
Type "What is Python?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Wait for the first assistant response to fully appear and streaming to stop (wait up to 45 seconds)
Wait 3 seconds for the UI to settle
Type "What are Python's main use cases?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Wait for the second assistant response to fully appear and streaming to stop (wait up to 45 seconds)
Wait 3 seconds for the UI to settle
Type "How does Python compare to JavaScript?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Wait for the third assistant response to fully appear and streaming to stop (wait up to 45 seconds)
Wait 5 seconds for suggestion generation to complete
Verify suggestion chips are visible below the last assistant message (pill-shaped buttons or a branch card with a git branch icon)
Click the dismiss button with aria-label "Dismiss suggestions" (the X icon button in the top-right corner of the suggestion area)
Verify the suggestion chips and branch suggestion card are no longer visible

title: Clicking a follow-up suggestion chip sends it as a message
description: Verifies that after creating a workspace and node from scratch and getting suggestions, clicking a follow-up chip sends it as a new chat message.

steps.description: Type "ChipTest Workspace" into the Title field
Click on the "ChipTest Workspace" card title to open it
Type "Python Overview" into the Title field
Verify text "Python Overview" is visible in the sidebar
Click on "Python Overview" in the sidebar to select the node
Verify the chat input with placeholder "Ask me anything ..." is visible
Type "What is Python?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Wait for the first assistant response to fully appear (wait up to 45 seconds)
Wait 3 seconds for the UI to settle
Type "What are Python's main use cases?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Wait for the second assistant response to fully appear (wait up to 45 seconds)
Wait 3 seconds for the UI to settle
Type "How does Python compare to JavaScript?" into the chat input and press Enter to send
Wait for the third assistant response to fully appear (wait up to 45 seconds)
Wait 3 seconds for suggestion generation to complete
Verify suggestion chips are visible below the last assistant message
Click the first visible follow-up suggestion chip (a pill-shaped button in the suggestion area)
Verify the text from the clicked chip appears as a new user message in the chat
Verify the suggestion chips are no longer visible after the chip was clicked
